steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls inside a dependent clause: ‘which often held symbolic ______ of birds, horses, and other animals.’ The blank must complete the noun phrase so that ‘significance’ and ‘depictions’ connect correctly within that clause — the clause can’t end mid-sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘significance. Depictions’: A period ends the sentence here, but the clause ‘which often held symbolic significance’ is still open — the dependent clause never closes. Fragments the sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘significance: depictions’: A colon introduces something that explains or lists what came before it. ‘Significance: depictions of birds’ implies depictions define significance, but the sentence already has a coordinating element (‘and inlaid enamel accents’) that parallels ‘depictions’ — a colon breaks that parallel structure. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘significance; depictions’: A semicolon here separates two parallel noun phrases within the list governed by ‘These included’: (1) intricate patterns … significance; depictions of birds … and (2) inlaid enamel accents. It correctly joins two closely related but distinct elements of Celtic art without fragmenting the sentence. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘significance, depictions’: A comma alone cannot separate two major list elements when the second element itself contains commas (‘birds, horses, and other animals’) — this creates an ambiguous run-on. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
